claim_support.reason: Most claims are supported by the cited evidence chunks. However, the financial growth rate claims (e.g., 7.33% YoY revenue growth) are mathematically inconsistent with the raw revenue figures provided in the cited evidence chunks (Q3 2025 $15.031b vs Q3 2024 $13.041b implies ~15.3% growth, not 7.33%). While the claims are supported by the cited Calculation IDs, those IDs are not internally consistent with the source data chunks, weakening the support validity.

numerical_accuracy.reason: Severe numerical errors in Year-over-Year (YoY) growth calculations. Q3 2025 Revenue YoY growth is stated as 7.33%, but the cited Q3 2024 and Q3 2025 revenue figures ($13.041b and $15.031b) imply ~15.3% growth. The 7.33% figure corresponds to Sequential growth (Q2 to Q3). Similarly, Q3 2025 Net Income YoY growth is stated as -12.43%, while the cited figures imply ~-16.2%. The -12.43% figure corresponds to Sequential growth. Q1 2025 Revenue YoY growth (26.65%) incorrectly compares Q1 2025 to Q3 2024. These are material misrepresentations of financial performance trends.

citation_precision.reason: Citations correctly link to the provided Authoritative cited chunk IDs and Calc IDs. The mismatch lies in the content of the Calc IDs relative to the Chunk IDs, not in the citation mapping itself.

temporal_validity.reason: All news and financial data dates are on or before the report As-of date of 2025-11-17. No post-as-of information is used.

consistency.reason: The report is internally consistent in repeating the same figures across sections (e.g., Q3 Revenue, Margins). The inconsistency exists between the text/calc IDs and the raw evidence chunks, which is captured under Numerical Accuracy and Claim Support.

financial_numeric.reason: Skipped because the report failed the Accuracy Gate due to severe unsupported claims, numerical errors.
